text.content: I run 2 AI agents with persistent memory and it costs exactly $0 for storage. Here's the stack. 🧵
The "expensive" way: vector databases, embeddings, semantic search. Pinecone, Weaviate, etc. Cool tech. Overkill for my use case.
My way: flat markdown files. That's it.

The structure:
• MEMORY.md — Long-term curated knowledge
• memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md — Daily raw logs
• TOOLS.md — Local config and credentials
• SOUL.md — Agent personality/identity
Why markdown works: LLMs already understand it. No embedding needed. Just load the file into context and go.
Syncthing keeps files in sync across machines. Git tracks history. Both free.
The "memory check protocol": before answering questions about past events, agents MUST search their memory files. Prevents hallucination.
Daily maintenance: every few days, agents review their daily logs and update MEMORY.md with what's worth keeping long-term.

Trade-offs? Yes. No semantic search. Context window limits matter. But for 90% of use cases, flat files + good discipline beats complex infra.

Start simple. Add complexity when you hit real limits—not imagined ones. #AIAgents #BuildInPublic
